name: Human cord plasma revitalized hippocampal function in aged mice
summary: Human cord plasma and TIMP2-related interventions improved hippocampal and cognitive readouts in aged mice.
statement: Human umbilical cord plasma improved aged mouse hippocampal plasticity and cognitive readouts, with TIMP2 identified as one candidate mediator of the effect.
population_or_model: Aged mice receiving human cord plasma or candidate plasma protein interventions
time_horizon: Repeated treatment followed by hippocampal and behavioral assays
quantitative_note: This finding supports tissue-specific animal plausibility for plasma-borne factors.
caveats: The result is animal evidence using human plasma material.
The active component and translational dosing strategy remain uncertain.
Mouse hippocampal effects do not establish human neurocognitive benefit.
